title: Wildwood featurette lifts the veil on building its stop-motion world
gist: Laika Studios released a behind-the-scenes featurette for its stop-motion adaptation of Wildwood, directed by CEO Travis Knight, with a large ensemble voice cast and an October 23, 2026 theatrical release; the piece is promotional culture coverage with essentially no investor-relevant signal.

insights.claim: Laika Studios' extended teaser for Wildwood, released in May, has racked up nearly 90 million views, and the studio has now released a behind-the-scenes featurette showing its stop-motion production process as a marketing tactic ahead of a full trailer.
insights.speaker: Jennifer Ouellette
insights.so_what: Strong early teaser engagement (90M views) is a leading indicator of consumer interest for Laika's theatrical slate, relevant to anyone tracking indie/animation studio box-office positioning ahead of the film's release.
insights.quote: Laika Studios released an extended teaser back in May, which has racked up nearly 90 million views.
insights.locator: Color us officially intrigued by Wildwood

insights.claim: Wildwood is directed by Laika Studios CEO Travis Knight, who also directed the Oscar-nominated Kubo and the Two Strings, and the studio optioned the film rights to Colin Meloy and Carson Ellis's 2011 novel shortly after its publication.
insights.speaker: Jennifer Ouellette
insights.so_what: Knight's dual CEO/director role and prior Oscar-nominated track record is the main quality signal for evaluating the film's commercial and awards prospects.
insights.quote: Knight is CEO of Laika Studios, which optioned the film rights for the novel shortly after publication.
insights.locator: Knight is CEO of Laika Studios

insights.claim: Wildwood is scheduled for theatrical release on October 23, 2026, with a voice cast including Peyton Elizabeth Lee, Jacob Tremblay, Carey Mulligan, Angela Bassett, and a large ensemble of additional actors in undisclosed roles.
insights.speaker: Jennifer Ouellette
insights.so_what: A concrete release date and A-list-adjacent ensemble cast size are the only tangible scheduling/production facts an entertainment-industry observer could log for tracking Laika's 2026 theatrical calendar.
